data_migration.notes: No parent migration is authorized; each child defines its own append-only artifacts.

risks: One giant PR would mix repository governance and runtime authority
Premature execution authority could mutate systems without valid evidence
Private data could leak during naive integration
approval_changes: No parent implementation approval is granted
Each child requires its own BUILD_NOW assessment, PASS preflight, and explicit approval
existing_assumptions: Hermes Drive remains thin orchestration layer
Skills and Packs keep separate canonical responsibilities
rationale: The parent crosses four independently testable boundaries: inventory/provenance, read-only evaluation, execution authority, and learning promotion. Direct implementation would recreate hidden-coupling risk.
rollback_boundary: Keep the parent FROZEN and NOT_APPROVED; remove no existing assets. Each child is independently revertible by its merge commit.
hidden_dependencies: private repositories cannot be copied into public RTS
provider adapters are not canonical records
